aggi 30 half three sign Center All right. All right, what's up? How are you doing? Good, very good. How's things? Good. Cool, I'm gonna have to. You're on the 20? I think the shorts are funny. I'm like upset, because I have no idea why I'm not supposed to be on the scale. Like, day-d, I'm on the scale, I'm on the scale, and I'm on the scale. So it's like, how is that, you know? Yeah, so, let us special, what happens if you lose a pound a day for the next year? You get weight negative 200, right? You can't lose a pound a day every day. You gotta understand a lot of that is what. And when people first begin this type of program, they go from eating just whatever, to eating fairly low, clogged diet, and clean. Low sodium, less processed food. So what happens is your water retention starts to drop dramatically. People usually hold a lot of water, a lot of inflammation, a lot of bloating. And when they start something like this right out of the bath, a lot of detox is cleansed out, and loses a lot of excess water. So it's not like you're losing a lot of fat in that. The first week, you're losing a lot of water. And eventually, one step stabilizes, like there's only so much you're gonna drop on that. Now it's gonna taper off and slow down, but when you start losing weight now, it's gonna be actually fat. So water comes off like a gain. So literally like they want to lose like three pounds of water, just because you're madly eating the table chips and cookies. So don't even get caught up on that. You gotta understand something. Prior to beginning anything like this, right? People are generally gaining weight every month or every year because no one is born here. You're small, you grow, you become an adult, and you're really young, but when you're 16, 17, you probably weigh less than you do today. And over time, bad habits get in control. At some point, and at this point, usually it's when they start, they make a U-turn. And they start going down. Now once you do that, it doesn't matter how fast that happens, because you're so young that all you gotta do is have this role right now is time. It's not like you're 70, and you gotta like hit a certain weight when you're 73. It doesn't matter, you're 20 years old. As long as you made that U-turn, and you're conscious you understand what you're doing, and you haven't found doing it, success is guaranteed. It's just a matter of how long you're gonna stay. They're fiber. They also give you some minerals, environmental stuff that you need, but they do not necessarily affect that much your body composition, which is what we're trying to tackle right now. Protein, carbs, and fats do. Veggies essentially are great for people that say, hey, I'm still hungry after my meal. More veggies will help to make you feel fuller. But I figured in the morning, I was feeling like I was eating a lot of food. So those meal plans are designed to give you what you need, but on a minimal basis. They're relatively deprived of energy or calories. They're fairly low, they have a pretty decent calorie deficit. When you're skipping them, you really, really put yourself in a deep hole, calorically. And that is going to be detrimental to a slow down in progress. Under-eating is just as bad and often worse, especially for men, than overeating. Because men rely on our muscle in order to keep the fat off. We have naturally more muscle than women do. And when we lose the ability to hold on to that muscle, the amount of factor we're able to burn or a basement of alkraic drops. And your ability to lose and keep fat off, with less muscle is going to be really bad, your energy is going to be really bad, you have to support the muscle.
